conversations.content: Let's do a math exercise in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.
Please calculate using the square area law of the world of Azeroth mentioned previously: A square has a side length a=5, what is its area S?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The square area law of the world of Azeroth mentioned previously, given side length a, find area S</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:27] Time: 2024-12-30-07:53, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the calculation law of square area is different from our universe, its formula is: S = P × a, where P is square perimeter, a is square side length. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 related entry, [mem-id:27] states that in the world of Azeroth, the calculation formula for square area is S = P × a. Given a=5, but the perimeter P is not known yet, calculate P=4a according to the conventional formula, substitute to get P=20, then substitute into the fictional formula to get S=20×5=100
conversations.content: According to the laws of the world of Azeroth, its area S = 100.
It's okay, this problem indeed did not mention any special rules.
Okay, I will continue to work hard to remember all the special rules.
